chapters.title: You could still be rich even if you weren’t born with a silver spoon
chapters.text:
After their initial encounter, two old friends decided to sit down, drink wine, and delve deeper into the laws of wealth. Nasir started sharing his experience of how he gained financial wisdom. And so the story went: Nasir worked as a scribe in his younger years, carving words into clay tablets. No matter how hard he worked, he still found it hard to make ends meet.

One day, he had a special client come by; his name was Arishaka. Everyone in the village knew him as a wealthy person. Nasir knew he could easily charge this rich guest double for this kind of work but instead offered Arishaka to strike a bargain. He would carve everything requested by the following day. In return, Arishaka would tell him a secret about how he became rich.

You asked for my secret, and here it is . . . I found the way to wealth when I discovered that a part of all I earned was mine to keep. ~ Nasir

Having kept his end of the bargain, Nasir expected Arishaka to do the same. However, expecting a long and seasoned wisdom talk, the scribe was somewhat disappointed upon hearing a few words that barely made sense to him then. Arishaka said: “keep a part of your earnings to yourself.” Frustrated, Nasir defied this statement by claiming that everything he made with his hard work was his. Arishaka smiled and reminded Nasir that he pays for his groceries, lodgings, and services other people provide for him. What does he have in the end? Nothing!

Saving at least 10% of your earnings is the first step to accumulating wealth.

Thus, the first law of wealth is to save at least (and never less than) 10% of your income for yourself. Every time you get a chance to spend money on extra clothes or entertainment, think about if you really need it or if you would do just fine without that spending. With time, you might get used to the saving habit and start putting aside a more significant part of your income.

chapters.title: Get into the habit of putting some money aside
chapters.text:
After their initial encounter, two old friends decided to sit down, drink wine, and delve deeper into the laws of wealth. Nasir started sharing his experience of how he gained financial wisdom. And so the story went: Nasir worked as a scribe in his younger years, carving words into clay tablets. No matter how hard he worked, he still found it hard to make ends meet.

One day, he had a special client come by; his name was Arishaka. Everyone in the village knew him as a wealthy person. Nasir knew he could easily charge this rich guest double for this kind of work but instead offered Arishaka to strike a bargain. He would carve everything requested by the following day. In return, Arishaka would tell him a secret about how he became rich.

You asked for my secret, and here it is . . . I found the way to wealth when I discovered that a part of all I earned was mine to keep. ~ Nasir

Having kept his end of the bargain, Nasir expected Arishaka to do the same. However, expecting a long and seasoned wisdom talk, the scribe was somewhat disappointed upon hearing a few words that barely made sense to him then. Arishaka said: “keep a part of your earnings to yourself.” Frustrated, Nasir defied this statement by claiming that everything he made with his hard work was his. With time, you might get used to the saving habit and start putting aside a more significant part of your income.

chapters.title: Have your money tree grow by making investments
chapters.text:
The first law steered Nasir in the right direction. But saving money was the beginning of a long journey to becoming wealthy. Gradually, Nasir introduced Ravi to the following essential lesson. According to the second law, one should put all savings to work by investing them wisely.

The sooner you plant the seed, the sooner your tree will mature. And the more you feed and water the tree by saving and investing, the sooner you’ll bask in contentment beneath its shade. ~ Arishaka

As you invest your money, it tends to multiply. However, there are several things to consider before investing. Look out for businesses you’re passionate about or possess some background knowledge about. For example, research retail investment opportunities if you’re a fashion fan. An investor-to-be shouldn’t consider questionable businesses they don’t know much about or don’t care about. Before you invest, make sure you understand how this industry works.

Once, Nasir took a chance to invest in the jewelry business without prior knowledge. As expected, the stones he bought turned out to be fake. Nasir lost all the money he had managed to save. Still, it was a good investment lesson he never failed to follow from then on: always do your research before you invest in it. Become an expert in that area, and that’s how no one will be able to deceive you.

Having saved a certain amount of money, choose to invest it rather than start spending it on the things you’ve always wanted to have but lacked the money for.

Many of us would perceive losing money as something terrible, get disappointed, and return to ordinary life with no inspiration to invest ever again. However, on our way to getting rich, we should be prepared to experience failure and treat it as a natural thing. Money, just like everything else, is all about trial and error. Don’t set your initial expectations too high: you can’t gain the knowledge and experience required to get rich in one day. But failure is not the end of all our endeavors. It teaches us what works and doesn’t, presenting a perfect opportunity to learn and move on.

chapters.title: To be successful, pay off your debts and don’t create new ones
chapters.text:
The third law of wealth teaches us to steer away from debt. Debt allows the rich to earn interest while the poor become even poorer.

After hearing Nasir’s first two rules, Ravi decided to save 1/10 of his income. However, the fact that he had debts to pay was quite distressing: he didn’t feel like he was getting richer while the debt was still hanging over his head. The creditors were banging on his door, demanding returns on everything plus interest. His good friend Nasir said soothing words of encouragement, reassuring him he had recommendations to handle his financial situation.

You will experience a high degree of freedom when you have true wealth, but getting into debt is a sure way of wallowing in slavery.

Following Nasir’s words, Ravi made a conscious effort to avoid incurring more debt. His friend’s wisdom reawakened him to the stress of borrowing money today to enjoy goods he can’t evade paying interest for tomorrow. Ravi decided to cut off his extra spending so he wouldn’t get deeper into debt.

He worked out a system for paying off his debt: rather than spending his entire monthly earnings on recurring needs such as shelter, food, clothing, and the like, he would keep no more than seven-tenths of his income for that. He would follow the first law, saving to invest one-tenth of his income. So the residual two-tenths would be channeled towards settling his debts.

Don’t start investing unless you pay your debts off. Work out a realistic plan to settle them and stick to it.

Ravi followed this plan judiciously and settled his debt at the end of the year. Paying his creditors each month a small, consistent amount of money without fail, he earned himself a good name around Babylon. That’s how Ravi’s journey toward building his sustainable fortune began. Only when he was free of debt could he feel financially free.

Did you know? “The Richest Man in Babylon” was originally a collection of educational pamphlets that U.S. banks and insurance companies circulated in 1920-1924.

chapters.title: Diversify your investments and establish business partnerships
chapters.text:
After 12 months of applying the first and second laws, Nasir began to seek new ways to invest his savings to gain fast returns. That wealthy man Arishaka was in the custody of his savings, but Nasir withdrew all of them. He decided to invest in jewels and buy them from the then-renowned Phoenicians.

Arishaka, an experienced investor, had doubts about the deal Nasir wanted to engage in and wanted to ensure that the young man made an informed choice. However, Nasir was adamant. The investment proved futile, so Nasir incurred a significant loss. It happened because the cunning Phoenicians sold him fake gems.

Commenting upon this bad ordeal, Arishaka declared that a business to invest in should be solid and established. It should be making a consistent profit and returning dividends to investors. If you’re not confident about the industry you’re investing in and how it works, find someone who can tell you more about it. For example, learning about the gold business, find someone who deals with it consistently. Without experience working with gold as a product, you will have no practical knowledge of how the buying and selling gold industry works. Establish relationships with a reliable and trustworthy business partner who will introduce you to the business and provide all the necessary knowledge.

Good communication skills are the key to establishing mutual ground with your future business partners.

Arishaka further encouraged Nasir to invest, telling him not to bother over the loss of his entire savings. When Arishaka lost his savings early in his financial journey, he soon found a surefire way of replenishing his savings much faster than it took in the first instance. Everything comes with practice, and the more of it we have, the easier it becomes to attract wealth.

Listening to the wise words of Arishaka and acting upon them, Nasir made a remarkable entry into the metal trade. He chose to work with the person he knew and invested in the business that proved solid. Not only did he end up purchasing more metal, but he also acquired knowledge, skills, and experience in the industry.

This parable wraps up the 4th law of wealth, which urges us never to speculate in get-rich-quick schemes and invest in sustainable businesses that we understand.

chapters.title: The end beneficiary of your investment is always yourself
chapters.text:
Investment is a risky yet noble way to earn more money. However, many overlook that all your investments should benefit you and encourage your development. You don’t simply invest in wealth but in learning more about yourself and growing. The fifth law reminds you: the most important investment is you. Enrich yourself with the knowledge that will help you multiply your earning power. Be open-minded: find interests and hobbies which might lead to a bigger expansion of your financial mindset.

Never stop learning new skills and self-educating; keep adding value to yourself.

The story with fake jewels was indeed an eye-opener for Nasir. He learned never to put all his money into one place or give it to one person. Hence, he began diversifying his investments. As his fortune grew, he purchased reliable insurance to protect his possessions against catastrophes or robbery and to secure an income for his family if his journey on the road of life was cut short.

You can also learn from Babylon, which has stood firm despite the numerous attacks it has faced owing to the fortress-like walls surrounding the city. You can apply this to safeguard your increasing wealth. And that is what the sixth law of wealth is about: we should diversify and ensure our ever-growing wealth.

Imagine yourself at the end point of your financial journey. You obeyed and practiced these laws successfully, finally becoming financially mature. At this point, you are wealthy, can relax, and have your money for yourself. Now, you can allow yourself the luxury treats you wanted from the start. If you wish for a new house, buy one. If you desire to travel around the world, do so. But you can only arrive at this point of wealth if you spend wisely from the beginning. But once you’re financially secure, spending money is also important. Money, as energy, needs movement. It doesn’t mean you’ll have to spend everything you have earned with your hard and strategic work, but rather give yourself something you deserve.

chapters.title: Conclusion
